evidence: Task builder contracts remain mixed-mode parameter bags: `JoinClipsTaskParams` carries legacy/new clip inputs, edit-mode fields, orchestration metadata, and UI restoration fields in one interface (`src/shared/lib/tasks/joinClips.ts:66-117`).

`IndividualTravelSegmentParams` combines source payload replay (`originalParams`), runtime overrides, identity/linkage, and deprecated SVI fields in a single input contract (`src/shared/lib/tasks/individualTravelSegment.ts:31-97`).

`buildTravelBetweenImagesPayload` still conditionally mutates a broad `Record<string, unknown>` output with many mode flags and optional arrays, rather than constructing from mode-specific typed payloads (`src/shared/lib/tasks/travelBetweenImages/payloadBuilder.ts:65-217`).

Consumers must probe multiple fallback shapes for the same semantic fields (`orchestrator_details`, `full_orchestrator_payload`, `individual_segment_params`, top-level), showing interface drift and weak contract honesty (`src/shared/components/TaskDetails/VideoTravelDetails.tsx:77-187`, `src/tools/travel-between-images/components/ShotImagesEditor/hooks/useShotImagesEditorCallbacks.ts:56-80`).

In `supabase/functions/calculate-task-cost/index.ts`, the orchestrator sub-task lookup captures `error` as `_subTasksError` and never checks it (`lines 291-301`), then silently falls back to single-task duration logic (`lines 322-327`).

In `supabase/functions/complete_task/orchestrator.ts`, most DB failures throw and fail the flow (`lines 157-163`, `431-437`), but billing failure only logs with `console.error` and still returns success (`lines 439-445`).

In `src/tools/travel-between-images/components/ShotImagesEditor/hooks/useShotImagesEditorCallbacks.ts`, adapter methods use mixed failure contracts: some paths throw via `handleAndRethrow` (`lines 170-175`, `184-189`, `199-204`), while capability-missing paths return sentinels (`false`/`undefined` at `lines 164-166`, `179-180`, `193-195`), and another capability-missing path throws (`lines 229-231`).
error_consistency.issues_preventing_higher_score: Cost/billing and callback layers still use inconsistent failure signaling (silent fallback, log-only failure, mixed sentinel-vs-throw contracts), which can hide real errors and make caller behavior unpredictable.

findings.summary: Task builders still use oversized mixed-mode param bags, forcing runtime branching instead of explicit mode contracts.
evidence: `JoinClipsTaskParams` includes legacy and new input shapes plus video-edit-only and orchestration fields in one type (`joinClips.ts:66-117`).
`IndividualTravelSegmentParams` accepts both replayed raw payload (`originalParams`) and override values for nearly every field (`individualTravelSegment.ts:43-97`).

`buildTravelBetweenImagesPayload` composes one mutable object with many conditional mode switches (`advanced_mode`, pair overrides, structure fields), rather than narrowing with discriminated unions (`payloadBuilder.ts:113-217`).
findings.suggestion: Split each task family into discriminated input contracts (for example `mode: 'legacy_join' | 'multi_clip' | 'video_edit'`, `source: 'orchestrator_replay' | 'manual_override'`) and route to mode-specific payload builders. Keep one adapter layer for backward compatibility, but isolate it at module boundaries and emit a single canonical payload schema.

findings.summary: Multiple consumers depend on fallback chains across duplicate payload paths, indicating a drifting, non-canonical task param interface.
evidence: `VideoTravelDetails` resolves the same logical fields from several locations (`individual_segment_params`, `orchestrator_payload`, `orchestrator_details`, top-level), including advanced-mode and prompt fields (`VideoTravelDetails.tsx:77-187`).

`useShotImagesEditorCallbacks` checks `pair_shot_generation_id` in FK column and two nested JSON paths (`params` + `individual_segment_params`) during deletion logic (`useShotImagesEditorCallbacks.ts:56-80`).

Orchestrator completion logic still reads expected segment counts from both nested and top-level keys (`orchestrator_details.num_new_segments_to_generate` and `num_new_segments_to_generate`) (`orchestrator.ts:45-61`).
findings.suggestion: Define and enforce a single versioned task-params schema per task family, then add a one-time normalization adapter when reading legacy rows. After normalization, downstream modules should read only canonical fields to remove fallback probing.

findings.summary: Thin pass-through exports remain that add import hops without policy or transformation.
evidence: `src/shared/lib/utils.ts` is a pure re-export of `cropFilename` and `truncateText` (`utils.ts:1`).
`useUpdatingTimestamp` is a direct alias to `useRelativeTimestamp` without additional behavior (`useUpdatingTimestamp.ts:42-44`).
findings.suggestion: Either remove these pass-throughs and migrate call sites to canonical modules, or convert them into intentional compatibility boundaries with explicit deprecation tags and removal timeline to keep indirection accountable.

findings.summary: Sub-task query errors are ignored and downgraded into normal-flow billing calculations.
evidence: `calculate-task-cost` assigns `error` to `_subTasksError` and never branches on it (`supabase/functions/calculate-task-cost/index.ts:291-301`).
When `subTasks` is empty (including error cases), code falls back to task-level duration (`supabase/functions/calculate-task-cost/index.ts:322-327`) instead of returning a recoverable billing error.
Upstream orchestrator completion relies on billing call outcomes (`supabase/functions/complete_task/orchestrator.ts:439-445`), so hidden query errors can propagate as incorrect cost records.
findings.suggestion: Handle `_subTasksError` explicitly: log structured context, return `errorResponse('subtask_query_failed', ...)` (recoverable), and avoid falling back to regular duration when orchestrator detection query fails.

findings.summary: Orchestrator completion treats billing failure as log-only while other failures are fatal.
evidence: Orchestrator DB update errors throw (`supabase/functions/complete_task/orchestrator.ts:431-437`), but billing errors only log (`supabase/functions/complete_task/orchestrator.ts:439-445`).

`calculate-task-cost` emits structured failure responses for operational issues (`supabase/functions/calculate-task-cost/index.ts`, multiple `errorResponse(...)` returns), but caller does not map `!ok` to task failure/retry state.
findings.suggestion: Unify post-completion billing failure semantics: either (a) throw/retry so orchestrator remains non-final until billing succeeds, or (b) persist an explicit `billing_pending`/`billing_failed` state and schedule retry instead of console-only logging.

findings.summary: Shot image editor callbacks mix throw-based and sentinel-based failure contracts for similar adapter operations.
evidence: Callback adapters in `useShotImagesEditorCallbacks` return `false`/`undefined` when handlers are absent (`lines 164-166`, `179-180`, `193-195`) but throw on runtime errors via `handleAndRethrow` (`lines 170-175`, `184-189`, `199-204`).
In the same hook, delete fallback in non-preloaded mode throws immediately (`lines 229-231`), creating a third behavior for capability-missing cases.

Related mutation hooks generally use throw/`handleAndRethrow` for DB operation failures (`src/shared/hooks/useGenerationMutations.ts`), so consumers can’t rely on a single callback error contract across adjacent flows.
findings.suggestion: Define one explicit callback contract (prefer `Promise<Result>` or consistent throw semantics) for add/create/delete adapters, and normalize missing-capability cases to the same contract rather than mixing sentinels and thrown errors.
